title: Add event logging to libpod, even display to podman
body:
In lipod, we now log major events that occurr.  These events
can be displayed using the `podman events` command. Each
event contains:

* Type (container, image, volume, pod...)
* Status (create, rm, stop, kill, ....)
* Timestamp in RFC3339Nano format
* Name (if applicable)
* Image (if applicable)

The format of the event and the varlink endpoint are to not
be considered stable until cockpit has done its enablement.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

> Missing completions.

why do you think that?

```
_podman_events() {
    local options_with_args="
     --help
     --h
     --filter
     --format
     --since
     --until
    "
    case "$cur" in
	-*)
	    COMPREPLY=($(compgen -W "$options_with_args" -- "$cur"))
	    ;;
    esac
}
```
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2491) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

I'm interested in understanding the behaviour here.

You mention Cockpit in the description, but the description sounds like it would add logging and display past events, not future ones (the bit that UIs are interested in). Or is `podman events` a blocking command that shows you new events as long as it's running? (i. e. a varlink call with `more`, which behaves like a signal notification)?

Thanks!
The goal is to have that correct.  Think of it like tail -f of the events log.
@martinpitt BTW You are the driving factor in adding this feature, so we want to make sure that it satisfies your needs/requirements.
